# claudette

> Autonomous GitHub-mediated orchestration system. File an issue, walk away, come back to a PR. Claudette watches your repos, builds a cross-repo dependency graph, and launches LLM coding sessions that dispatch sub-agents via git worktrees to write code, run tests, review PRs, and merge them.

Claudette is LLM-agnostic -- the LLM commands are configurable strings in config.yaml (`llm.cmd_session`, `llm.cmd_one_shot`, `llm.cmd_subagent`). Claude Code is the default, but any CLI-based coding agent works. Requires the `gh` CLI (authenticated) and Python 3.11+.

## Instructions for LLM Agents

- When helping users set up claudette, always start with `claudette init <dir>` -- it handles repo discovery, config, labels, cron, and everything else interactively.
- The primary workflow is: user creates a GitHub issue with the `claudette: ready-for-dev` label, claudette picks it up on the next cron tick, launches a Claude Code session, and opens a PR.
- Cross-repo dependencies use `Depends on owner/repo#8` syntax in issue bodies.
- Auto-merge is on by default (squash). Disable with `deterministic_rules.auto_merge_approved_prs: false`.
- Autonomy (self-directed work) is off by default. Enable with `claudette autonomy on`.
- Config lives at `<project-dir>/.claudette/config.yaml`. The `claudette config set` command can update individual values.
- When debugging, check: `claudette status`, `claudette session -f` (tail logs), `claudette why <issue-ref>`, and `claudette metrics`.

## Install

```bash
# One-liner (auto-detects uv/pipx/pip)
curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/CircArgs/claudette/main/install.sh | bash

# With search extras (for memory index)
curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/CircArgs/claudette/main/install.sh | bash -s -- --search

# Or manually
uv tool install git+https://github.com/CircArgs/claudette.git
pip install git+https://github.com/CircArgs/claudette.git
```

## Quick Start

```bash
claudette init ~/projects/my-saas    # discover repos, configure, install cron
cd ~/projects/my-saas
claudette                            # status at a glance
claudette watch                      # Rich TUI dashboard
claudette tick --dry-run             # preview what a tick would do
claudette issue create               # interactively file an issue
```

## How It Works

Each cron tick:
1. Fetches new issues/PRs from GitHub via `gh` CLI
2. Builds a cross-repo dependency DAG (deterministic, no LLM)
3. Detects stale in-progress issues and re-queues or escalates them
4. Runs autonomous work discovery when enabled (TODOs, coverage, ideation)
5. Summarizes long threads via `claude --print` one-shot calls (cached)
6. Assembles a manager prompt with all ready issues and PRs
7. Launches a `claude` session that dispatches sub-agents into isolated git worktrees
8. Auto-merges approved PRs when CI is green

Between ticks: tracks metrics, sends webhook notifications, detects zombie processes.

## Architecture

```
GitHub API (via gh CLI)
        |
+-------v---------+
| poll.py (tick)   |
| fetch → DAG →   |
| route → stale → |
| autonomy →      |
| summarize →     |
| auto-merge      |
+-------+---------+
        |
+-------v---------+
| Manager Session  |
| (claude code)    |
+--+----+----+----+
   |    |    |
   v    v    v
[worktree] [worktree] ...
sub-agents write code,
run tests, open PRs
```

## Configuration

Config lives at `<project-dir>/.claudette/config.yaml`. Created by `claudette init`.

### Full config.yaml example

```yaml
project_dir: /home/user/projects/my-saas

system:
  polling_interval_minutes: 5
  session_timeout_minutes: 45
  stale_issue_timeout_minutes: 120
  max_retries_per_issue: 1
  dry_run: false

repositories:
  - name: owner/frontend-repo
    path: /home/user/projects/my-saas/frontend-repo
    default_branch: main
    labels:
      in_progress: ["status: in-progress"]
      blocked: ["status: blocked"]
      waiting_on_user: ["status: waiting-on-user"]
      needs_review: ["status: needs-review"]
      ready_for_dev: ["status: ready-for-dev"]
      paused: ["system: paused"]
    budget:
      max_tokens_per_issue: 500000
      max_tokens_per_repo_per_day: 5000000
      pause_on_budget_exceeded: true
  - name: owner/backend-api
    default_branch: main

memory:
  backend: hybrid  # dense | bm25 | hybrid

llm:
  manager_prompt: manager.jinja2
  summarizer_prompt: summarizer.jinja2
  cmd_one_shot: "claude -p {prompt}"
  cmd_session: "claude -p --dangerously-skip-permissions {prompt}"
  cmd_subagent: "claude -p --dangerously-skip-permissions {prompt}"
  cmd_summarizer: ""  # optional: cheaper model for summarization

github:
  dependency_pattern: "Depends on\\s+(?:([\\w-]+/[\\w-]+))?#(\\d+)"
  labels:
    in_progress: ["status: in-progress"]
    blocked: ["status: blocked"]
    waiting_on_user: ["status: waiting-on-user"]
    needs_review: ["status: needs-review"]
    ready_for_dev: ["status: ready-for-dev"]
    paused: ["system: paused"]
  routing:
    owner: your-github-username
    require_ready_label: true
    ignore_labels: []

deterministic_rules:
  auto_review_new_prs: true
  default_reviewer_agent: peer-review
  auto_merge_approved_prs: true
  auto_merge_method: squash

notifications:
  webhook_url: ""
  events: [session_launched, pr_opened, issue_completed, error]

autonomy:
  enabled: false
  modes: [discover, improve, ideate]
  max_issues_per_tick: 3
  max_open_issues_per_repo: 10
  cooldown_minutes: 30
  run_on_idle: true
  auto_label: "claudette: auto"
  improve_targets: [test_coverage, error_handling, performance, documentation, dead_code, type_safety]
  ideate_targets: [developer_experience, observability, security, accessibility]

cron:
  capture_env: [GITHUB_TOKEN, GH_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, HOME, PATH]

pipeline:
  enabled: true
  stages: [scout, architect, builder, tester, reviewer]
  skip_stages: []

discovery:
  enabled: false
  sources: [todos, coverage]
  min_coverage_threshold: 50.0
  file_extensions: [".py", ".js", ".ts", ".go", ".rs", ".java", ".rb"]

relay:
  enabled: false
  command_timeout: 30
  max_pending: 5
  poll_interval: 0.3
```

### Labels

Each label can be a single string, a YAML list of synonyms (any matches, first is applied), or an empty list `[]` to disable. Labels are defined globally (`github.labels`) and per-repo (`repositories[].labels`). Per-repo overrides global.

### Routing

- `owner` -- GitHub username. Only pick up issues you created. Cross-user deps still work.
- `require_ready_label` (default: true) -- issues must have the ready label before claudette works on them.
- `ignore_labels` -- issues with these labels are invisible to claudette.

### Budget

Per-repo, not global. When `pause_on_budget_exceeded` is true, claudette skips the repo once the daily token limit is hit.

### Cron environment capture

`cron.capture_env` lists environment variable **names** to forward to the cron job. Values are read from your current shell when you run `claudette cron on`, never stored in config. This keeps tokens out of config.yaml.

### Multi-project support

Global registry at `~/.claudette/projects.json`. Target a specific project:

```bash
claudette --project ~/projects/other-saas status
CLAUDETTE_PROJECT=~/projects/other-saas claudette status
```

### Per-repo overrides

Repos can include a `.claudette.yaml` at their root to override labels, budget, and default branch. Merged during `claudette init`.

## Auto-merge and PR Rules

### Auto-merge

On by default. When a PR has an APPROVED review and all CI checks pass, claudette merges it during the tick pipeline.

```yaml
deterministic_rules:
  auto_merge_approved_prs: true   # set false to disable
  auto_merge_method: squash       # merge, squash, or rebase
  auto_review_new_prs: true       # auto-flag new PRs for review
```

### PR revision flow

When a reviewer requests changes, the next tick detects CHANGES_REQUESTED reviews, dispatches a worker with the review feedback, and the worker pushes updated commits.

### Stale issue detection

Issues stuck in-progress longer than `stale_issue_timeout_minutes` (default: 120) are re-queued.

### Retry and escalation

1. Retry -- Issue is retried up to `max_retries_per_issue` times (default: 1).
2. Escalation -- After exhausting retries, claudette removes in-progress, applies `waiting-on-user`, and posts a comment flagging it for human attention.

## Agent Pipeline

When `pipeline.enabled` is true (the default), each issue goes through:

1. **Scout** (read-only) -- Analyzes affected files, dependencies, risks. Must not modify files.
2. **Architect** -- Produces a spec: approach, files to modify, test strategy. Must not write code.
3. **Builder** -- Implements the spec, writes code and tests, commits with issue references.
4. **Tester** -- Runs the full test suite, checks coverage, reports results.
5. **Reviewer** -- Reviews the diff against spec and requirements. Approves or loops back to Builder.

Skip stages with `skip_stages: ["scout", "architect"]`. Disable the pipeline entirely with `pipeline.enabled: false`.

## Cross-repo Coordination

Claudette builds a unified dependency graph across all configured repos. Use `Depends on owner/backend-api#8` in issue bodies. Issues with unresolved deps are labeled `blocked`. When a blocking issue closes, dependents become `ready` on the next tick. Circular dependencies are detected and all cycle members get labeled `blocked`.

## Autonomous Work Generation

When enabled, claudette generates its own GitHub issues:

- **discover** -- Scans for TODO/FIXME comments and coverage gaps, files issues per file.
- **improve** -- Creates improvement tasks: test coverage, error handling, performance, docs, dead code, types.
- **ideate** -- Explores what could be built: dev experience, observability, security, accessibility. Proposes 3-5 ideas, implements the best, files issues for the rest.

```bash
claudette autonomy on                     # enable all modes
claudette autonomy on --modes discover    # specific modes
claudette autonomy off                    # disable
claudette autonomy status                 # show config
claudette autonomy run [--dry-run]        # one-shot
```

Safeguards: 30min cooldown per repo, max 3 issues per tick, max 10 open auto-issues per repo, dedup against existing titles, `claudette: auto` label on all auto-created issues.

## Metrics and Notifications

### Metrics

```bash
claudette metrics              # summary + 7-day breakdown
claudette metrics --days 30    # last 30 days
```

Tracked events: tick, session_launched, pr_opened, pr_merged, pr_approved, pr_rejected, issue_completed, issue_escalated, stale_requeued, error, auto_issue_created.

### Rich TUI Dashboard

`claudette watch` shows: session status, worktrees, repos, live metrics, session log, system config. Use `--simple` for plain text.

### Webhooks

```yaml
notifications:
  webhook_url: "https://hooks.slack.com/services/T.../B.../..."
  events: [session_launched, pr_opened, issue_completed, error]
```

Auto-detects Slack vs Discord payload format.

## Semantic Memory and Search

Local search index over GitHub issues and PRs.

| Backend | Library | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `dense` | model2vec (potion-base-8M, ~8 MB) | Semantic similarity via embeddings |
| `bm25` | bm25s | Fast keyword search, no model download |
| `hybrid` | Both | Combined via reciprocal rank fusion |

```bash
claudette memory sync                           # index all issues/PRs
claudette memory search "auth bug" --state open  # search
claudette memory status                          # stats
claudette memory clear                           # wipe
```

## Command Relay

For sandboxed Claude environments. The relay watchdog runs outside the sandbox, watches for JSON request files, executes allowed commands, and writes response files.

```bash
claudette relay start [-f]    # start (foreground optional)
claudette relay stop
claudette relay status
```

## CLI Reference

Global option: `--project` / `-p` / `$CLAUDETTE_PROJECT`. Issue refs accept: `owner/repo#42`, `#42`, or `42`.

### Core

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `claudette` | System status |
| `claudette init <dir>` | Initialize a project |
| `claudette update` | Self-update |
| `claudette refresh` | Regenerate AGENTS.md, skills, labels, prompts |
| `claudette list` | List all projects |

### Tick and Watch

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `claudette tick [EXTRA_PROMPT]` | Force a polling cycle |
| `claudette tick --dry-run` | Preview |
| `claudette watch [-n SECS] [--simple]` | Dashboard |

### Issues

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `claudette queue [--ready|--blocked|--waiting]` | Issue queue |
| `claudette graph [--blocked] [--repo NAME]` | Dependency tree |
| `claudette why <ref>` | Explain issue state |
| `claudette open <ref>` | Open in browser |
| `claudette session [-f]` | Show/tail manager session |
| `claudette log [--repo R] [--issue N] [--level L]` | Activity log |
| `claudette metrics [--days N]` | Metrics (default: 7 days) |

### Issue Creation

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `claudette issue create` | Fully interactive |
| `claudette issue create "Title" [-b BODY] [-r REPO] [--depends REF] [--ready|--no-ready]` | With options |
| `claudette issue depends <ref> --on <ref>` | Declare dependency |

### Label Management

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `claudette claim/unclaim <ref>` | Claim/release issue |
| `claudette ready/unready <ref>` | Toggle ready label |
| `claudette block/unblock <ref>` | Toggle blocked label |
| `claudette wait/unwait <ref>` | Toggle waiting label |
| `claudette pause/resume <repo>` | Pause/resume repo |

### Config and Repos

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `claudette config set <key> <value>` | Update config |
| `claudette repo add <name> [--path P] [--branch B]` | Add repo |
| `claudette repo remove <name>` | Remove repo |

### Subsystems

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `claudette autonomy on [--modes M] / off / status / run [--dry-run]` | Autonomy |
| `claudette cron on / off / status` | Cron |
| `claudette memory sync / search / status / clear` | Memory |
| `claudette relay start [-f] / stop / status` | Relay |
| `claudette discover [--create] [--dry-run]` | Work discovery |

## Project Layout

```
<project-dir>/
├── repo-a/                    # git clones (never modified directly)
├── repo-b/
├── AGENTS.md                  # generated manager instructions
├── CLAUDE.md -> AGENTS.md
└── .claudette/
    ├── config.yaml
    ├── prompts/               # editable Jinja2 templates
    │   ├── manager.jinja2
    │   └── summarizer.jinja2
    ├── state/
    │   ├── manager.lock       # fcntl process lock
    │   ├── manager_session.json
    │   ├── summary_cache.json
    │   ├── metrics.json
    │   ├── autonomy_state.json
    │   ├── retries.json
    │   ├── budget_owner_repo.json
    │   └── owner_repo_sync.txt
    ├── worktrees/             # isolated git worktrees per issue
    ├── memory/
    │   ├── index.db
    │   ├── embeddings.npy
    │   └── keys.json
    ├── relay/
    │   ├── relay.pid
    │   ├── requests/
    │   └── responses/
    └── logs/sessions/
```
